title: Add Repairs by Stage and Top Failing Models charts to Dashboard

requirements.text: Add a backend query function that aggregates repair tickets by their current stage/status and returns a count per stage (e.g., Awaiting Parts, Closed, Deployed, Diagnosing, Programming, QA Testing, Ready Deploy, Received, Repairing).

quotes: the data should come from real records stored in the backend

acceptanceCriteria: A query function exists in backend/main.mo that iterates all repair tickets and groups them by stage, returning an array of (stage, count) pairs.
All nine stages are represented in the output (with count 0 if no tickets exist for that stage).

requirements.text: Add a backend query function that aggregates repair tickets by device model and returns the top failing models sorted by repair count descending (top 5 minimum: VX680, VX820, M400, Carbon 10, Carbon 8).

quotes: the data should come from real records stored in the backend

acceptanceCriteria: A query function exists in backend/main.mo that counts repair tickets per device model and returns them sorted by count descending.
Returns at least the top 5 models by repair frequency.

requirements.text: Add a TanStack Query hook in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts for fetching repairs-by-stage aggregation data from the new backend query function.
acceptanceCriteria: A useRepairsByStage hook exists that calls the backend aggregation query and returns stage/count data.
The hook follows the same pattern as existing query hooks in useQueries.ts.

requirements.text: Add a TanStack Query hook in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts for fetching top-failing-models aggregation data from the new backend query function.
acceptanceCriteria: A useTopFailingModels hook exists that calls the backend aggregation query and returns model/count data.
The hook follows the same pattern as existing query hooks in useQueries.ts.

requirements.text: Add a 'Repairs by Stage' vertical bar chart to the existing DashboardPage (frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx) using Recharts BarChart. The bars must be green (#10b981 or similar), X-axis labels must show all nine stage names rotated at an angle for readability (matching the screenshot: AWAITING PARTS, CLOSED, DEPLOYED, DIAGNOSING, PROGRAMMING, QA TESTING, READY DEPLOY, RECEIVED, REPAIRING), and the Y-axis must show repair counts. The chart must be displayed in a card with the title 'Repairs by Stage' and pull live data via the useRepairsByStage hook.

quotes: Repairs by Stage: a vertical bar chart (green) showing repair counts across stages

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Repairs by Stage' bar chart is visible on the dashboard page.
Bars are rendered in green.
All nine stage names appear on the X-axis, rotated for readability.
Chart data reflects live counts from actual backend repair ticket records.
Chart is wrapped in a styled card with the title 'Repairs by Stage'.
Chart displays a loading skeleton while data is being fetched.

requirements.text: Add a 'Top Failing Models' horizontal bar chart to the existing DashboardPage (frontend/src/pages/DashboardPage.tsx) using Recharts BarChart with layout='vertical'. The bars must be orange (#f59e0b or similar), Y-axis labels must show model names (VX680, VX820, M400, Carbon 10, Carbon 8), and the X-axis must show repair counts. The chart must be displayed in a card with the title 'Top Failing Models' and pull live data via the useTopFailingModels hook.

quotes: Top Failing Models: a horizontal bar chart (orange) showing the top failing device models such as VX680, VX820, M400, Carbon 10, Carbon 8

acceptanceCriteria: The 'Top Failing Models' horizontal bar chart is visible on the dashboard page.
Bars are rendered in orange.
Model names appear on the Y-axis.
Chart data reflects live counts from actual backend repair ticket records.
Chart is wrapped in a styled card with the title 'Top Failing Models'.
Chart displays a loading skeleton while data is being fetched.

requirements.text: Lay out the two new charts side-by-side in a two-column grid row on the DashboardPage, matching the layout shown in the uploaded screenshot, positioned below the existing KPI cards and above the recent tickets/assets tables.

quotes: add these two charts to the existing dashboard

acceptanceCriteria: Both charts appear in the same horizontal row on the dashboard.
The layout uses a two-column grid (each chart taking roughly equal width).
On smaller screens the layout stacks the charts vertically.
The charts are positioned below KPI cards and above the recent records tables.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Use Recharts for all chart rendering, consistent with the existing ReportsPage.tsx implementation.
Both charts must support dark/light mode using existing Tailwind dark mode class conventions.
nonGoals: Adding additional dashboard widgets beyond the two charts specified.
Modifying existing KPI cards or recent records tables on the dashboard.
Changing the backend data model or existing repair ticket fields.
